0.job_summary: Catapult Sports is seeking a Principal Firmware Engineer / Team Lead based in Melbourne to lead a firmware development team building wearable tracking hardware for elite sports. The role involves developing Linux kernel and wireless firmware for ARM Cortex platforms, bringing up new hardware from scratch, and driving DevOps practices and engineering processes. The position requires a player-coach leadership style, including mentoring team members, partnering with product managers, and promoting technical strategy and innovation. The ultimate goal is to deliver high-quality firmware solutions for wearable and stadium infrastructure products used by over 4,600 professional sports teams worldwide.

education_requirements.requirement: Formal qualifications in electronics or software engineering

experience_requirements.requirement: 5-10 years of firmware development experience
Experience with Linux (Yocto/PetaLinux), including kernel building, custom kernels, device trees, and Linux drivers
Experience bringing up new hardware from scratch
Experience with wireless communications standards and protocols (BLE, Wi-Fi, UWB, long range/low bit rate)
Experience developing, scaling, and supporting products throughout the entire lifecycle
Experience communicating with users, technical teams, and product management
Experience with Atlassian platform for defect tracking and documentation
Experience with Git code management
Team lead or engineering leadership experience

skill_requirements.requirement: C and C++ programming
Python programming
Strong verbal and written communication skills
Organizational and time management skills
Ability to work with minimal specifications and be proactive
Understanding of high performance engineering and how to drive it
Rust programming or willingness to learn
Occasional after-hours or weekend technical customer support

0.red_flags: Occasional weekend and after-hours technical support required, which may indicate an expectation of availability outside standard working hours. The role spans a very broad scope including team leadership, hands-on firmware development, DevOps, architecture planning, and customer support, which could indicate an understaffed team or unrealistic expectations for a single role.

1.job_summary: The System Development Manager will support AWS Region Services Operations in Australia, working in a DevOps capacity to develop and deploy operational tools that automate processes and reduce overhead. The role involves hiring, developing, and mentoring team members while collaborating with engineers to build and manage massively scaled distributed services. The candidate will participate in 24/7 on-call rotations, respond to high-severity events, and present solutions to senior leadership. Additionally, the role requires data collection, analytics, and dashboard creation to drive business improvement across AWS infrastructure.

experience_requirements.requirement: 5+ years managing system or software development teams
7+ years of hands-on systems engineering and administration in networking, storage, and operating systems
7+ years leading definition and development of multi-tier web services
Experience partnering with product or program management teams
Experience with AWS platforms, services, and design patterns
Experience communicating with users, technical teams, and senior leadership

skill_requirements.requirement: Linux proficiency (distros, package management, permissions, logging, auditing)
Linux command-line tools (grep, awk, sed, ip, ifconfig, tcpdump, strace, etc.)
DNS and related tools (bind, host, dig, nslookup)
Runlevel and service management (systemd, rc.d, chkconfig)
System monitoring tools (top, stat tools, etc.)
Git or other source control management tools
Agile development practices
CI/CD and development pipelines
Full software/hardware/network development lifecycle knowledge
Ability to obtain Australian Government Security Vetting Agency (AGSVA) clearance
Distributed systems design and delivery understanding
Risk assessment and management
Professional software engineering best practices and coding standards
Data collection, analytics, and dashboard creation
Strong communication and presentation skills for senior leadership
Knowledge of systems engineering fundamentals (networking, storage, OS)

1.red_flags: The role requires participation in a 24/7 on-call rotation for high-severity issues, which may significantly impact work-life balance despite the company's stated commitment to work-life harmony. A government security clearance (AGSVA) is mandatory, which can be a lengthy and intrusive process and may restrict the eligible candidate pool. The breadth of the role — spanning development, operations, people management, senior leadership presentations, and on-call responsibilities — suggests a potentially high-pressure and demanding workload.

2.job_title: Delivery Consultant – Software Engineer (AWS Professional Services)
2.job_summary: AWS Professional Services is seeking a hands-on Delivery Consultant with deep software engineering and cloud architecture expertise to deliver complex technical consulting engagements for customers across Australia and New Zealand. The role involves designing and developing cloud-native solutions, implementing CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices, and building distributed systems using AWS services. The consultant will provide technical leadership to customer and partner teams, guiding architectural decisions and ensuring solution quality and security. A strong focus is placed on innovation using AWS AI/ML services, including building AI-enabled applications and agent architectures. The position combines customer-facing consulting with deep hands-on software development responsibilities.

experience_requirements.requirement: 5+ years of programming in at least one modern language (C++, C#, Java, Python, Golang, PowerShell, Ruby)
5+ years leading development of applications backed by AWS or other cloud-based services
5+ years of SDLC and agile/iterative methodologies building full stack applications
Experience with Infrastructure as Code (AWS CDK, CloudFormation, Terraform) and CI/CD pipelines
Experience with distributed systems, microservices architecture, and event-driven patterns at large scale
Experience with containerization and orchestration technologies (Docker, Amazon ECS, Kubernetes)
Experience with observability and monitoring tools (OpenTelemetry, Datadog, Splunk, Prometheus, Grafana)

skill_requirements.requirement: Cloud-native solution design and development using AWS services
Software development across full stack applications and modern development methodologies
DevOps practices including CI/CD pipeline implementation and infrastructure as code
Technical leadership including code reviews, architectural guidance, and best practices
AI/ML application development including agent architectures and multi-agent systems
API design and implementation (REST, GraphQL, gRPC) and identity protocols (OpenID Connect, OAuth 2.0)
Knowledge of RAG patterns, vector databases, embedding pipelines, and prompt engineering
Understanding of AI agent protocols (MCP, A2A) and development patterns

2.red_flags: The role blends deep hands-on software engineering with customer-facing consulting, which may be a challenging balance for candidates who prefer one or the other. The breadth of preferred qualifications — spanning AI/ML, observability, containerisation, API design, and identity protocols — is extensive and may suggest unrealistic expectations. No salary or compensation range is provided, which limits transparency for prospective applicants.

3.job_summary: The Automation Test Engineer will utilize Accenture's proprietary Codeless Test Automation Suite (CTAS) to develop and execute automated test scripts. The role involves collaborating with development teams to ensure software meets quality standards while managing and maintaining the CTAS framework. The engineer will provide technical support to the team and contribute to problem-solving initiatives. This position offers opportunities for career growth and engagement with innovative technologies within a large global consulting firm.

education_requirements.requirement: Bachelor's Degree in a relevant field of study

experience_requirements.requirement: 1-2 years of experience in relevant related skills

skill_requirements.requirement: Codeless Test Automation Suite (CTAS) development and execution
Automated test script development
CTAS framework management and maintenance
Technical support and team collaboration
LLMOps at a Master proficiency level

3.red_flags: The role requires expertise in Accenture's proprietary CTAS platform, which is an internal tool, meaning candidates are unlikely to have prior experience with it and may face a steep learning curve. The job description is notably vague about specific responsibilities and day-to-day tasks, offering little concrete detail about the actual work involved. The LLMOps "Master" proficiency preference seems mismatched for a role requiring only 1-2 years of experience.

4.job_summary: Orikan is seeking a Graduate Full Stack Developer to join their Early Careers program, working on enterprise-grade web applications using the Microsoft .NET ecosystem. The role involves both front-end and back-end development, including building APIs, business logic, and responsive interfaces. Graduates will work alongside senior developers, designers, and product managers while gaining exposure to the full software development lifecycle. The position is based at Orikan's Melbourne Head Office with flexible working options, and includes structured mentoring and hands-on experience with production systems.

education_requirements.requirement: Completed degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field

experience_requirements.requirement: Exposure to ASP.NET Core through study or projects
Exposure to Microsoft Azure or cloud platforms
Familiarity with Git and version control workflows
Awareness of CI/CD pipelines and deployment practices
Exposure to unit or automated testing

skill_requirements.requirement: Strong foundational knowledge of C# and Object-Oriented Programming
Understanding of HTML, CSS and JavaScript
Basic knowledge of SQL / relational databases
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication skills and collaborative mindset
Australian Permanent Residency or Citizenship
Understanding of RESTful API design and integration

4.red_flags: Role requires Australian Permanent Residency or Citizenship, which significantly limits applicant eligibility including international graduates. Salary is not disclosed at any stage of the listing.
